title: QUIC CRT client-side ClientHello PSK-attempt architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest PSK-attempt seam: the managed client/runtime path consumes dormant detached resumption material to build a PSK-capable ClientHello with binder emission while keeping early data closed and without claiming resumed-handshake success yet.
risks: If the binder is emitted without the dormant carrier actually feeding the ClientHello attempt path, the slice would look implemented while remaining dead code.
If the detached carrier is consumed without preserving the closed early-data gate or the bootstrap fallback, the slice would overclaim resumption capability that the repository has not yet proven.
If the pre_shared_key ordering or binder-length math is wrong, the server parser will reject the ClientHello and the attempt path will not be truthful.
If the carrier leaks into public API or becomes part of the supported IsSupported promise, the slice will widen beyond the narrow proof floor.
design_summary: The managed client/runtime path already owns opaque ticket ingress, a runtime-local ticket snapshot, a detached carrier handoff, and the minimum resumption credential material. This slice consumes that dormant carrier to construct a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt with the supported `psk_dhe_ke` mode, a single PSK identity, a computed binder, and an explicitly closed early-data gate. The carrier stays immutable and dormant outside the emitted ClientHello, and the slice does not claim resumed-handshake success or any public surface change.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only client-side ClientHello PSK-attempt construction from the dormant detached carrier and the minimum parser acceptance needed to ingest the emitted PSK-attempt message. It does not add resumed-handshake success, 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or a public API expansion.
